title: Expand to 7+ reviewed non-electrical categories (add general-mechanical + 1 more)
context: The M1 core toolkit delivered 5 non-electrical triage checklists (furniture/wood, bikes, ceramics/gluing, jewelry/watches, toys). To reach the M3 target of 7+ reviewed non-electrical categories, at least 2 more categories must be authored and reviewed. The PLAN identifies general-mechanical (non-mains items, e.g. battery-powered or manual tools/appliances) and one additional partner-driven category. Category priority should be informed by research-017 (partner input) once available. All units must follow the same citation, license, and review governance as prior content.
objective: Author and review at least 2 additional non-electrical triage checklist categories (including general-mechanical and one more, ideally partner-driven), bringing the total reviewed non-electrical categories to 7 or more, with each unit conforming to the data-002 schema and passing CI governance gates.
acceptanceCriteria: At least 2 new non-electrical triage checklist categories are delivered, including general-mechanical (non-mains battery or manual items); the total reviewed non-electrical categories (including those from content-007) reaches at least 7
Each new unit covers: is-it-worth-fixing assessment, common faults, tools needed, safe-handling notes, and when-to-stop criteria

Every factual claim in each new unit is source-cited (retrievedDate, sourceLicense, reusePermitted); no verbatim copying of copyrighted or NC-licensed text; no third-party logos; no 'Repair Café' name
Each unit is reviewed by an approver distinct from the author; a PR-tied review-log entry is written for each unit
All new units render from the data-002 schema, pass CI content-governance gates, print legibly, and are published under CC-BY-4.0
output: At least 2 committed, schema-valid additional non-electrical triage checklist units (CC-BY-4.0), including general-mechanical, bringing the reviewed category total to 7+, each with source-cited claims and distinct reviewer/approver review-log entries.
